name: atlas-pipeline-ssot
description: ATLAS pipeline SSOT template: preserve locked truth, write YAML, repair, validate, handoff

tasks.detail: Read existing locked truth files under <ip>/req before the first SSOT write when they exist, especially approval_manifest.json, requirements_index.json, obligations.json, contract_refs.json, structural_contracts.json, behavioral_contracts.json, and evidence_plan.json. Do not write or update canonical req/*.json authority files. Use locked truth as authority, or the orchestrator goal as starter input only when no locked truth exists, to write <ip>/yaml/<ip>.ssot.yaml as a generator-ready Design Spec projection. Preserve the canonical SSOT sections, but add custom.locked_truth_authority when req/approval_manifest.json exists and attach source_refs/contract_refs/evidence_refs to important section items. If structural_contracts.json exists, project its generic IO/clock/reset/interface signals into io_list exactly, including direction, width, clock/reset domains, and sync/async timing. If behavioral_contracts.json exists, project decision tables, transactions/state rules, and stage checks into function_model, cycle_model, fsm/register behavior, test_requirements, and quality_gates. If the user explicitly wants direct RTL before FL/CL, record quality_gates.rtl_gen.direct_rtl_allowed with approval rationale instead of silently skipping model artifacts. Include top_module, sub_modules, parameters, io_list, features, dataflow, function_model, cycle_model, registers, interrupts, fsm, custom.assumptions, test_requirements, quality_gates, traceability, and workflow_todos. Keep the first write compact enough to complete quickly; repair_ssot_schema.py can expand canonical sections after disk evidence exists.
tasks.criteria:
<ip>/yaml/<ip>.ssot.yaml is written by a real file-write tool
The first YAML is a Design Spec projection, not canonical req authority
If req/approval_manifest.json exists, custom.locked_truth_authority cites it and its bundle_sha256
If req/structural_contracts.json exists, io_list ports match its name/dir/width/timing and carry structural contract_refs
If req/behavioral_contracts.json exists, function_model/cycle_model/test behavior carries behavioral contract_refs and machine rules
Important interface/register/model/test items carry source_refs and contract_refs
Non-reset function_model transactions include output_rules or state_updates
Submodules include ownership refs
Assumptions are recorded under custom.assumptions
No RTL/TB/sim/lint/coverage/EDA artifacts are written by ssot-gen

tasks.detail: Run python3 "$ATLAS_WORKFLOW_ROOT/ssot-gen/scripts/repair_ssot_schema.py" <ip> --root "$ATLAS_PROJECT_ROOT" --mode engineering, then python3 "$ATLAS_WORKFLOW_ROOT/ssot-gen/scripts/verify_ssot.py" <ip> --root "$ATLAS_PROJECT_ROOT" --mode engineering. If <ip>/req/approval_manifest.json exists, also run python3 "$ATLAS_WORKFLOW_ROOT/ssot-gen/scripts/check_design_spec_trace.py" <ip> --root "$ATLAS_PROJECT_ROOT". Inspect failures literally and repair the YAML if needed.
tasks.criteria:
repair_ssot_schema.py executed
verify_ssot.py executed with --mode engineering
check_design_spec_trace.py executed when locked truth exists
YAML parses successfully
Preview fields are present or explicitly waived
Locked truth projection trace passes or is reported as a blocker
Locked behavioral contracts are not anchor-only: function_model and cycle_model rows carry behavioral contract_refs plus machine-readable semantics or explicit cycle waivers
Validation output is captured
Any remaining failure is reported as a blocker, not green

tasks.detail: Emit a compact [SSOT HANDOFF] for rtl-gen. Include the exact SSOT path, top module, clock/reset, interfaces, registers/CSR policy, function/cycle model summary, locked truth authority status, assumptions, validation evidence, unresolved gaps, and downstream owners.
tasks.criteria:
[SSOT HANDOFF] block is emitted
Handoff names rtl-gen as next owner
Handoff does not claim RTL, TB, sim, lint, waveform, coverage, synthesis, STA, PnR, or signoff completion
